settings.title: Event Coverage for the Lantern Night Market
settings.summary: We covered a two-night street market and delivered a press-ready gallery within 24 hours.
settings.description:
The Lantern Night Market draws thousands over two evenings, and the organizers needed images they could send to press and sponsors right away. Coverage of an event like this is worth most while it is still fresh, so a gallery that arrived a week later would have missed the window.

The brief

Two things had to be true at once: the photos had to capture the scale and the feel of a packed night market, and they had to be edited and ready almost immediately afterward. Speed could not come at the cost of the images that would actually get used.

How we covered it

We worked the crowd, the vendors, and the stage across both nights, moving to cover the moments that tell the story of the market: the lanterns lit, the food stalls busy, the audience in front of the stage. We shot for variety so press, sponsors, and the organizers each had frames that suited them.

  • Crowd and atmosphere shots across both evenings
  • Vendor and food-stall coverage
  • Stage and performance frames
  • A curated, press-ready edit

The real work was the turnaround. We culled and edited overnight after each session and delivered a curated gallery the next morning, which meant making selection calls fast without dumping every frame on the client.

The result

The organizers had press-ready photos within 24 hours, while the market was still the talk of the week. Several shots ran in local coverage and in the sponsors' recap posts, putting the images to work exactly when they carried the most weight.
